compiled_state.title: As an engineer, I can invoke /dso:respond-to-pr-comments to classify and respond to all unresolved PR comments on a PR
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A new /dso:respond-to-pr-comments SKILL.md and entry point that orchestrates the full comment-response pipeline: invokes pr-comment-response.sh to fetch normalized comments, classifies each comment using an embedded LLM prompt, and dispatches the accept/defend/defer handler for each.
**Why**: Engineers need an on-demand command to address pending PR comments without waiting for the merge flow. The standalone skill provides a direct entry point and is the shared pipeline used by both standalone invocation and merge-to-main.sh integration.
**Scope**:
- IN: Skill entry point accepting a PR URL or PR number; invocation of pr-comment-response.sh to fetch normalized comments; embedded LLM classification prompt in SKILL.md that classifies each comment as: accept (engineer requests a specific, actionable code change), defend (the existing design is justifiable with codebase evidence — the prompt must output both the classification label AND the defense argument text to pass to Story 3's handler), or defer (out-of-scope, ambiguous, or blocked — default for any ambiguous case); per-comment dispatch to the appropriate action handler (Stories 2, 3, or 4); printed summary table of results (comment ID, classification, action taken) after all comments are processed; message "No unresolved comments found on PR #<n>." when pr-comment-response.sh returns empty
- OUT: human_in_loop confirmation flow (Story 5e); merge-to-main.sh integration (Story 6); response to the engineer via the PR itself (handled by action handlers)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running /dso:respond-to-pr-comments <PR-URL-or-number> fetches all unresolved comments, classifies them, dispatches handlers, and prints a summary table of comment ID, classification, and action taken
  ← Satisfies: "accepts a PR URL or number; otherwise prints a summary table of comment ID, classification, and action taken" (SC6)
- When this story is complete, the skill prints "No unresolved comments found on PR #<n>." when pr-comment-response.sh returns an empty comments array
  ← Satisfies: "when no unresolved comments exist, prints 'No unresolved comments found on PR #<n>.'" (SC6)
- When this story is complete, the embedded classification prompt classifies comments as accept, defend, or defer using the defined criteria; ambiguous comments default to defer; for defend cases, the prompt outputs both the classification label and the defense argument text (codebase-backed justification)
  ← Satisfies: "maps engineer comment intent to accept (actionable requested change), defend (design decision justifiable with codebase evidence), or defer (out-of-scope, ambiguous, or blocked); ambiguous comments default to defer" (SC8) + adversarial review finding RT-3 (defense argument text must be produced here)
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← TDD Done-of-Done requirement

## Considerations

- [Testing] Embedded prompt classification is LLM-powered — test suite must include deterministic classification smoke tests using pre-classified JSON fixtures (known-good accept/defend/defer examples) that do not require a live LLM call; use a mock classification path or hardcoded test fixture
- [Reliability] Concurrent invocations on the same PR could both fetch comments before either has posted replies — the <!-- dso-agent-reply --> sentinel provides deduplication for sequential runs but does not prevent double-processing within concurrent runs; document as a known limitation and consider a file-based advisory lock warning in the skill output
- [Maintainability] New skill entry point requires registration in CLAUDE.md Quick Reference table (done by Story D — this story creates the skill file, Story D documents it)
- [Maintainability] Classification prompt must output defense argument text (not just the label "defend") for defend-classified comments — this text is passed directly to Story 3's handler as the PR review comment body

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
